content: Agent-Orchestrated Answering Ensembles can be structured as a "dynamic working memory offloading system" to bypass individual agent working memory capacity limits that constrain reasoning. The orchestration layer can partition long-horizon reasoning tasks across the ensemble such that each agent only holds the small, contextually relevant chunk of working memory it needs for its specialized sub-task, rather than forcing a single agent to retain the entire reasoning chain.
source_topics: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
